1668	\begin{lemma}
1669	
1670	\leanok
1671	[Finiteness transports across a ring isomorphism and a semilinear additive map]
1672	  \label{lem:module_finite_of_ringEquiv_semilinear}
1673	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.module_finite_of_ringEquiv_semilinear}
1674	  Let \(\sigma : R \xrightarrow{\sim} R'\) be a ring isomorphism and
1675	  \(e : M \xrightarrow{\sim} M'\) an additive isomorphism that is \(\sigma\)-semilinear,
1676	  i.e. \(e(r \cdot m) = \sigma(r) \cdot e(m)\) for all \(r \in R\), \(m \in M\). If
1677	  \(M\) is a finite \(R\)-module then \(M'\) is a finite \(R'\)-module.
1678	\end{lemma}
1679	
1680	\begin{proof}
1681	  Pick a finite \(R\)-spanning set \(T \subseteq M\). The image \(e(T)\) is finite, and
1682	  it \(R'\)-spans \(M'\): for \(m' \in M'\) write \(m' = e(m)\) with
1683	  \(m = \sum_i r_i t_i\) (\(t_i \in T\)); then
1684	  \(m' = e(\sum_i r_i t_i) = \sum_i \sigma(r_i)\, e(t_i)\) by semilinearity, an
1685	  \(R'\)-combination of \(e(T)\). Hence the top submodule of \(M'\) is finitely
1686	  generated, i.e. \(M'\) is a finite \(R'\)-module.
1687	\end{proof}

1689	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1690	[G1 base case — finite affine sections from a finite generating family]
1691	  \label{lem:gf_qcoh_finite_sections_of_genSections}
1692	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gf_qcoh_finite_sections_of_genSections}
1693	  \uses{lem:gf_qcoh_sections_free_epi, lem:qcoh_affine_isIso_fromTildeΓ,
1694	        lem:module_finite_of_ringEquiv_semilinear}
1695	  Let \(X\) be a scheme, \(\F : X.\mathrm{Modules}\) a quasi-coherent sheaf of modules, and
1696	  \(D \subseteq X\) an affine open. Suppose the restriction
1697	  \(\F|_D = (\mathrm{pullback}\,D.\iota)^{*}\F\) carries a finite generating family
1698	  \(\sigma\), with index set \(I = \sigma.I\). Then the section module
1699	  \(\Gamma(\F, D)\) is a finite module over \(\Gamma(X, D)\). This is the affine base
1700	  case of \(G1\): once the generators are in hand on an affine open, finiteness of
1701	  sections is purely an affine (Spec-side) statement. (Only quasi-coherence of \(\F\) is
1702	  needed — the finite-generation datum is carried by \(\sigma\) directly, so no separate
1703	  finite-type hypothesis on \(\F\) is required.)
1704	\end{lemma}
1705	
1706	\begin{proof}
1707	  \uses{lem:gf_qcoh_sections_free_epi, lem:qcoh_affine_isIso_fromTildeΓ,
1708	        lem:module_finite_of_ringEquiv_semilinear, lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen}
1709	  The content is the transport of the finite generating datum
1710	  from the category of sheaves of modules over the affine open \(D\) to the affine
1711	  scheme \(\Spec \Gamma(X, D)\), where the affine base case applies. Write
1712	  \(R = \Gamma(X, D)\) and use the canonical identification
1713	  \(D \cong \Spec R\) (\(\mathrm{isoSpec}\)). The argument has three steps.
1714	
1715	  \emph{(a) Quasi-coherence on \(\Spec R\).} Transport \(\F|_D\) along
1716	  \(\mathrm{isoSpec}^{-1}\) to a sheaf of modules \(\F'\) on \(\Spec R\). Finite type of
1717	  \(\F\) is inherited by the restriction, and quasi-coherence of \(\F'\) is the assertion
1718	  that its counit \(\F'.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}\) is an isomorphism; this is the affine
1719	  quasi-coherence criterion \cref{lem:qcoh_affine_isIso_fromTildeΓ} fed by the basic-open
1720	  localization datum \cref{lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen}.
1721	
1722	  \emph{(b) A finite free epimorphism over \(\Spec R\).} The generating family \(\sigma\)
1723	  furnishes an epimorphism \(\OO_D^{\oplus I} \twoheadrightarrow \F|_D\). Transporting along
1724	  \(\mathrm{isoSpec}^{-1}\) and using that the pullback of the free module along an
1725	  isomorphism is again free (\((\mathrm{pullback}\,\mathrm{isoSpec}^{-1})^{*}\OO_D^{\oplus I}
1726	  \cong \OO_{\Spec R}^{\oplus I}\)), together with the identification of the free sheaf with
1727	  the associated sheaf \(\widetilde{N}\) of \(N = R^{\oplus I}\) (the associated-sheaf
1728	  functor preserves coproducts and \(\widetilde{R} \cong \OO_{\Spec R}\)), gives an
1729	  epimorphism \(\widetilde{N} \twoheadrightarrow \F'\) with \(N = R^{\oplus I}\) a finite
1730	  \(R\)-module (\(I\) finite).
1731	
1732	  \emph{(c) Identification of section modules.} The affine global-sections functor sends
1733	  \(\F'\) to \(\Gamma(\F', \top)\), which the transport identifies with \(\Gamma(\F, D)\)
1734	  as \(R = \Gamma(X, D)\)-modules.
1735	
1736	  Now the affine base case \cref{lem:gf_qcoh_sections_free_epi}, applied to the finite-free
1737	  quasi-coherent epimorphism \(\widetilde{N} \twoheadrightarrow \F'\) over \(\Spec R\),
1738	  shows \(\Gamma(\F', \top)\) is a finite \(R\)-module; by (c) this is \(\Gamma(\F, D)\)
1739	  finite over \(\Gamma(X, D)\).
1740	\end{proof}

1742	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1743	[G1 — finite-type quasi-coherent sheaf has finite affine sections]
1744	  \label{lem:gf_qcoh_fintype_finite_sections}

1746	  \uses{lem:gf_finite_sections_of_basicOpen_finite_cover,

1956	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1957	[B1.0 — localization commutes with base tensor]
1958	  \label{lem:gf_localizedModule_baseChange_tensor_comm}
1959	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gf_localizedModule_baseChange_tensor_comm}
1960	  Let \(R \to B\) be a ring map, \(T \subseteq B\) a submonoid, \(N\) a \(B\)-module,
1961	  and \(K\) an \(R\)-module. There is an isomorphism, natural in \(K\),
1962	  \[
1963	    \mathrm{LocalizedModule}\,T\,(N \otimes_R K)
1964	      \;\cong\; (\mathrm{LocalizedModule}\,T\,N) \otimes_R K .
1965	  \]
1966	\end{lemma}
1967	\begin{proof}
1968	  Localization is base change: \(\mathrm{LocalizedModule}\,T\,M \cong T^{-1}B \otimes_B M\)
1969	  naturally in the \(B\)-module \(M\). Applying this to \(M = N \otimes_R K\) and using
1970	  associativity/commutativity of the tensor product over the tower
1971	  \(R \to B \to T^{-1}B\) gives
1972	  \(T^{-1}B \otimes_B (N \otimes_R K) \cong (T^{-1}B \otimes_B N) \otimes_R K
1973	  \cong (\mathrm{LocalizedModule}\,T\,N) \otimes_R K\); naturality in \(K\) is inherited
1974	  from the tensor functor.
1975	\end{proof}

1977	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1978	[B1 — localizing the source ring preserves flatness over the base]
1979	  \label{lem:gf_flat_localizedModule_sameBase}
1980	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gf_flat_localizedModule_sameBase}
1981	  \uses{lem:gf_localizedModule_baseChange_tensor_comm}
1982	  Let \(R \to B \to N\) be a scalar tower in which \(N\) is a \(B\)-module, and let
1983	  \(T \subseteq B\) be a submonoid of the \emph{source} ring \(B\). View \(N\) as an
1984	  \(R\)-module through the tower. If \(N\) is flat over \(R\), then the localized module
1985	  \(\mathrm{LocalizedModule}\,T\,N\) is again flat over \(R\). \emph{(Note: the existing
1986	  Mathlib results \cref{lem:mathlib_flat_localization_preserves} localize over a
1987	  submonoid of the \emph{base} \(R\); here the submonoid lives in the source \(B\) while
1988	  the base \(R\) is untouched, so a separate argument is required.)}
1989	\end{lemma}
1990	
1991	\begin{proof}
1992	  \uses{lem:gf_localizedModule_baseChange_tensor_comm}
1993	  Localization at \(T\) commutes with tensoring against an \(R\)-module: by
1994	  \cref{lem:gf_localizedModule_baseChange_tensor_comm}, for every \(R\)-module \(K\)
1995	  there is a natural isomorphism
1996	  \[
1997	    \mathrm{LocalizedModule}\,T\,(N \otimes_R K)
1998	      \;\cong\; (\mathrm{LocalizedModule}\,T\,N) \otimes_R K,
1999	  \]
2000	  natural in \(K\) (compatible with the maps induced by any \(R\)-linear \(K \to K'\)).
2001	  Flatness of \(N\) over \(R\) means that for every injection \(K \hookrightarrow K'\) of
2002	  \(R\)-modules the induced map \(N \otimes_R K \to N \otimes_R K'\) is injective.
2003	  Localization at the submonoid \(T\) is an exact functor, so it preserves this
2004	  injectivity; transporting along the natural isomorphism turns the result into
2005	  injectivity of
2006	  \((\mathrm{LocalizedModule}\,T\,N) \otimes_R K \to (\mathrm{LocalizedModule}\,T\,N) \otimes_R K'\).
2007	  As this holds for every injection of \(R\)-modules, \(\mathrm{LocalizedModule}\,T\,N\)
2008	  is flat over \(R\).
2009	\end{proof}

2023	\begin{lemma}\leanok
2024	[B2.1 — cross-chart basic-open identity]
2025	  \label{lem:gf_crossChart_basicOpen_eq}
2026	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gf_crossChart_basicOpen_eq}
2027	  \uses{lem:mathlib_scheme_basicOpen_res}
2028	  Let \(X\) be a scheme, \(W, W_j \subseteq X\) two opens with overlap
2029	  \(O = W \sqcap W_j\), and let \(g \in \Gamma(X, W)\), \(\bar g \in \Gamma(X, W_j)\) be sections
2030	  that restrict to the \emph{same} section over \(O\), i.e.\ \(g|_O = \bar g|_O\) in
2031	  \(\Gamma(X, O)\). Suppose both basic opens lie in the overlap,
2032	  \(X.\mathrm{basicOpen}\,g \le O\) and \(X.\mathrm{basicOpen}\,\bar g \le O\). Then they agree as
2033	  opens of \(X\):
2034	  \[
2035	    X.\mathrm{basicOpen}\,g \;=\; X.\mathrm{basicOpen}\,\bar g .
2036	  \]
2037	  In particular the open \(D(g)\) is computed identically whether read in the chart \(W\) or in
2038	  the chart \(W_j\), and \(D(g) = D(\bar g) \le W_j\) (no module content is involved).
2039	\end{lemma}
2040	
2041	\begin{proof}
2042	  \uses{lem:mathlib_scheme_basicOpen_res}
2043	  Basic opens are compatible with restriction (\cref{lem:mathlib_scheme_basicOpen_res}). For the
2044	  inclusion \(O \le W\), \(X.\mathrm{basicOpen}\,(g|_O) = O \sqcap X.\mathrm{basicOpen}\,g\), and
2045	  since \(X.\mathrm{basicOpen}\,g \le O\) the meet collapses to
2046	  \(X.\mathrm{basicOpen}\,(g|_O) = X.\mathrm{basicOpen}\,g\). Symmetrically, for \(O \le W_j\),
2047	  \(X.\mathrm{basicOpen}\,(\bar g|_O) = O \sqcap X.\mathrm{basicOpen}\,\bar g
2048	  = X.\mathrm{basicOpen}\,\bar g\). The hypothesis \(g|_O = \bar g|_O\) makes the two left-hand
2049	  sides equal, so \(X.\mathrm{basicOpen}\,g = X.\mathrm{basicOpen}\,\bar g\).
2050	\end{proof}

2052	\begin{lemma}\leanok
2053	[B2.2 — two-leg section-localization transport]
2054	  \label{lem:gf_section_localization_twoleg}
2055	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gf_section_localization_twoleg}
2056	  \uses{lem:gf_crossChart_basicOpen_eq, lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen,
2057	        lem:gf_qcoh_fintype_finite_sections}
2058	  In the situation of \cref{lem:gf_flat_locality_assembly}, fix an affine open
2059	  \(W \le p^{-1}(U)\), a patch \(W_j = \Spec B_j\) with \(M_j = \Gamma(\F, W_j)\), and a matched
2060	  pair \(g \in \Gamma(X, W)\), \(\bar g \in B_j = \Gamma(X, W_j)\) cutting out the same basic open
2061	  \(D := X.\mathrm{basicOpen}\,g = X.\mathrm{basicOpen}\,\bar g \le W \sqcap W_j\) (as produced by
2062	  \cref{lem:gf_crossChart_basicOpen_eq}). Then the single group \(\Gamma(\F, D)\) carries two
2063	  localized-module structures:
2064	  \begin{enumerate}
2065	    \item the section restriction \(\Gamma(\F, W) \to \Gamma(\F, D)\) exhibits \(\Gamma(\F, D)\) as
2066	      \((\mathrm{powers}\,g)^{-1}\,\Gamma(\F, W)\); and
2067	    \item the section restriction \(M_j \to \Gamma(\F, D)\) exhibits \(\Gamma(\F, D)\) as
2068	      \((\mathrm{powers}\,\bar g)^{-1}\,M_j = (M_j)_{\bar g}\).
2069	  \end{enumerate}
2070	  Consequently \((\mathrm{powers}\,g)^{-1}\,\Gamma(\F, W) \cong (M_j)_{\bar g}\) canonically, by
2071	  uniqueness of localized modules.
2072	\end{lemma}
2073	
2074	\begin{proof}
2075	  \uses{lem:gf_crossChart_basicOpen_eq, lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen,
2076	        lem:gf_qcoh_fintype_finite_sections}
2077	  By \cref{lem:gf_crossChart_basicOpen_eq} the two recipes for the open agree,
2078	  \(D = X.\mathrm{basicOpen}\,g = X.\mathrm{basicOpen}\,\bar g\), so \(\Gamma(\F, D)\) is one and the
2079	  same group whether \(D\) is read in chart \(W\) or in chart \(W_j\). Applying the quasi-coherent
2080	  section-localization datum \cref{lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen} with ambient affine \(W\)
2081	  and element \(g\), the restriction \(\Gamma(\F, W) \to \Gamma(\F, D)\) is the localization at
2082	  \(\mathrm{powers}\,g\), giving (1). Applying the same datum with ambient affine \(W_j\) and element
2083	  \(\bar g\) (legitimate since \(D = X.\mathrm{basicOpen}\,\bar g \le W_j\)), the restriction
2084	  \(M_j \to \Gamma(\F, D)\) is the localization at \(\mathrm{powers}\,\bar g\), giving (2). Both maps
2085	  localize their respective sources onto the same target \(\Gamma(\F, D)\); the universal property of
2086	  localized modules (\(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule.iso}\) uniqueness) supplies the canonical isomorphism
2087	  \((\mathrm{powers}\,g)^{-1}\,\Gamma(\F, W) \cong (M_j)_{\bar g}\). Finiteness of \(M_j\) over \(B_j\)
2088	  is recorded by \cref{lem:gf_qcoh_fintype_finite_sections}.
2089	\end{proof}

2091	\begin{lemma}\leanok

2093	  \label{lem:gf_base_localization_comparison}

2098	  \(f\), and the restriction \(A_f \to R := \Gamma(S, U)\) realises \(R\) as a localization of \(A_f\)
2099	  (both are rings of fractions of the domain \(A\) inside its fraction field, \(R\) inverting at least
2100	  the denominators already inverted in \(A_f\)).

2106	  \(A = \Gamma(S, U_0) \to \Gamma(S, D(f))\) exhibits \(A_f = \Gamma(S, D(f))\) as the
2107	  away-localization of \(A\) at \(f\) (\cref{lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib}). The affine open
2108	  \(U \le D(f) = \Spec A_f\) of the integral scheme \(S\) has section ring
2109	  \(R = \Gamma(S, U) \subseteq \mathrm{Frac}(A)\) containing \(A_f\); the comparison
2110	  \(A_f \to R\) is the restriction along \(U \le D(f)\) and exhibits \(R\) as a localization of
2111	  \(A_f\) inside the fraction field of the domain \(A\).

2121	  \(g \in \Gamma(X, W)\) with \(D(g) \subseteq W \cap W_j\). Then:
2122	  \begin{enumerate}

2124	      \(\Gamma(\F, D(g))\) as the away-localization
2125	      \((\mathrm{powers}\,g)^{-1}\,\Gamma(\F, W)\) of the source module
2126	      \(\Gamma(\F, W)\) at \(g\); and
2127	    \item independently of the chart, \(\Gamma(\F, D(g)) \cong (M_j)_{\bar g}\) is the
2128	      localization of the patch module \(M_j = \Gamma(\F, W_j)\) at the image

2131	  Moreover the base restriction \(\Gamma(S, U) \to A_f\) identifies \(\Gamma(S, U)\) and
2132	  \(A_f\) as localizations of \(A\) (both inverting the relevant denominators of the

2142	  transport \cref{lem:gf_section_localization_twoleg} then yields statements~(1) and~(2)

2148	  inside \(\mathrm{Frac}(A)\).

2157	  affine opens of \(X\) whose union contains \(W\). Then there is a finite index set \(K\) and, for

2171	  Inside each overlap \(W \cap W_j\) — open in both affines \(W\) and \(W_j\) — the opens that are
2172	  simultaneously basic in \(W\) and basic in \(W_j\) form a basis of the subspace topology: given
2173	  \(x \in W \cap W_j\), choose \(b \in \Gamma(X, W_j)\) with \(x \in D(b) \subseteq W \cap W_j\) and
2174	  then \(g \in \Gamma(X, W)\) with \(x \in D(g) \subseteq D(b)\); shrinking \(b\) to the common open
2175	  and recording its restriction over the overlap produces a basic open of \(W\) that is matched, over
2176	  \(W \cap W_j\), by a section \(\bar g \in \Gamma(X, W_j)\) (\cref{lem:gf_crossChart_basicOpen_eq}
2177	  certifies \(D(g) = X.\mathrm{basicOpen}\,\bar g\)). Ranging over all \(j\), and because the \(W_j\)
2178	  cover \(W\), these common basic opens form an open cover of the affine \(W\). The affine \(W\) is
2179	  quasi-compact, so a finite subfamily already covers it: applying the standard-subcover extraction
2180	  \cref{lem:gf_affine_finite_standard_subcover} to this open cover of \(W\) returns a finite family
2181	  \(\{g_k\} \subseteq \Gamma(X, W)\) with \(\mathrm{Ideal.span}\,\{g_k\} = \top\), each \(D(g_k)\) a

2186	\begin{lemma}[G3 — flat-locality assembly from per-patch freeness]
2187	  \label{lem:gf_flat_locality_assembly}
2188	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gf_flat_locality_assembly}
2189	  \uses{lem:gf_patch_free_imp_flat, lem:gf_flat_localizedModule_sameBase,
2190	        lem:gf_section_localization_flat_descent, lem:gf_crossChart_spanning_cover,
2191	        lem:mathlib_flat_of_isLocalized_span,
2192	        lem:mathlib_flat_localization_preserves, lem:mathlib_flat_of_free,
2193	        lem:gf_qcoh_fintype_finite_sections, lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen}
2194	  Let \(p : X \to S\) be a finite-type morphism with \(S\) integral and locally
2195	  noetherian, and \(\F\) a finite-type quasi-coherent \(\OO_X\)-module. Fix a non-empty
2196	  affine open \(U_0 = \Spec A \subseteq S\) with \(A\) a noetherian domain, a finite
2197	  affine cover \(\{W_j = \Spec B_j\}_j\) of \(p^{-1}(U_0)\), and an element
2198	  \(f \in A\), \(f \ne 0\), such that for every \(j\) the localized section module
2199	  \((M_j)_f\) (with \(M_j = \Gamma(\F, W_j)\)) is free over \(A_f\). Set
2200	  \(V = D(f) \subseteq U_0\). Then for every affine open \(U \le V\) of \(S\) and every
2201	  affine open \(W \le p^{-1}(U)\) of \(X\), the section module \(\Gamma(\F, W)\) is flat
2202	  over \(\Gamma(S, U)\).
2203	\end{lemma}
2204	
2205	\begin{proof}
2206	  \uses{lem:gf_patch_free_imp_flat, lem:gf_flat_localizedModule_sameBase,
2207	        lem:gf_section_localization_flat_descent, lem:gf_crossChart_spanning_cover,
2208	        lem:mathlib_flat_of_isLocalized_span,
2209	        lem:mathlib_flat_localization_preserves, lem:mathlib_flat_of_free,
2210	        lem:gf_qcoh_fintype_finite_sections, lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen}
2211	  Flatness is established by a \emph{source-span} descent on the affine \(W\): instead of
2212	  testing stalkwise, we descend along a finite cover of \(W\) by basic opens aligned to
2213	  the patches.
2214	
2215	  Fix affine \(U \le V = D(f)\) and \(W \le p^{-1}(U)\), and write \(R = \Gamma(S, U)\),
2216	  \(B = \Gamma(X, W)\), \(N = \Gamma(\F, W)\); \(N\) is a \(B\)-module and \(B\) an
2217	  \(R\)-algebra. By \cref{lem:gf_section_localization_flat_descent}, \(R\) is a
2218	  localization of \(A_f\).
2219	
2220	  \emph{A spanning family of basic opens.} By the patch-aligned covering lemma
2221	  \cref{lem:gf_crossChart_spanning_cover}, applied to the affine \(W\) and the finite affine cover
2222	  \(\{W_j\}\) of \(p^{-1}(U) \supseteq W\), there is a finite family
2223	  \(\{g_k\} \subseteq \Gamma(X, W)\) with \(\mathrm{Ideal.span}\,\{g_k\} = \top\) (so the \(D(g_k)\)
2224	  cover \(W\)), where each \(D(g_k) \subseteq W \cap W_{j(k)}\) is a common basic open matched by a
2225	  patch element \(\bar g_k \in B_{j(k)}\). The chosen \(g_k\) span the unit ideal of \(B\).
2226	
2227	  \emph{Each cover piece is flat over \(R\).} Fix such a \(g\), lying over the patch
2228	  \(W_j\). The per-patch freeness gives \((M_j)_f\) free, hence flat over \(A_f\)
2229	  (\cref{lem:gf_patch_free_imp_flat}, \cref{lem:mathlib_flat_of_free}); localizing the base
2230	  from \(A_f\) to \(R\) preserves flatness (\cref{lem:mathlib_flat_localization_preserves}),
2231	  so the corresponding module is flat over \(R\). Localizing this further at the source
2232	  submonoid \((\mathrm{powers}\,\bar g) \subseteq B_j\) keeps \(R\)-flatness by the
2233	  source-localization lemma \cref{lem:gf_flat_localizedModule_sameBase}. Since
2234	  \(D(g) \subseteq W \cap W_j\) lies over \(U \le V = D(f)\), the image of \(f\) is already
2235	  invertible in \((B_j)_{\bar g}\); hence localizing \((M_j)_f\) further at
2236	  \((\mathrm{powers}\,\bar g)\) inverts nothing new on the base side and
2237	  \((M_j)_f\) localized at \(\bar g\) coincides with
2238	  \((M_j)_{\bar g} = \mathrm{LocalizedModule}\,(\mathrm{powers}\,\bar g)\,M_j\)
2239	  (transitivity of localization). By
2240	  \cref{lem:gf_section_localization_flat_descent} this module is exactly
2241	  \(\Gamma(\F, D(g)) \cong (M_j)_{\bar g}\). Hence each \(\Gamma(\F, D(g))\) is flat over
2242	  \(R\).
2243	
2244	  \emph{Span descent.} By \cref{lem:gf_section_localization_flat_descent} each
2245	  \(\Gamma(\F, D(g))\) is the away-localization of \(N = \Gamma(\F, W)\) at \(g\), and the
2246	  \(g\)'s span the unit ideal of the source ring \(B\). The source-span flatness criterion
2247	  \cref{lem:mathlib_flat_of_isLocalized_span}, with base \(R\), source \(B\), and these
2248	  flat principal localizations, yields that \(N = \Gamma(\F, W)\) is flat over
2249	  \(R = \Gamma(S, U)\), as claimed.
2250	\end{proof}

2258	[Generic flatness, geometric form]
2259	  \label{thm:generic_flatness}
2260	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.genericFlatness}
2261	  \uses{thm:generic_flatness_algebraic}
2262	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S4, Theorem ``generic flatness''
2263	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1781-L1787).
2264	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
2265	  % "Let \(S\) be a noetherian and integral scheme.
2266	  %  Let \(p: X\to S\) be a finite type morphism, and let \(\F\) be a
2267	  %  coherent sheaf of \({\mathcal O}_X\)-modules. Then there exists a non-empty
2268	  %  open subscheme \(U\subset S\) such that the restriction of \(\F\)
2269	  %  to \(X_U = p^{-1}(U)\) is flat over \({\mathcal O}_U\)."
2270	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4, Theorem ``generic flatness''.}
2271	  Let \(S\) be a noetherian integral scheme, \(p : X \to S\) a finite-type morphism,
2272	  and \(\F\) a coherent \(\OO_X\)-module. Then there exists a non-empty open
2273	  subscheme \(U \subseteq S\) such that the restriction of \(\F\) to
2274	  \(X_U = p^{-1}(U)\) is \(\OO_U\)-flat.
2275	
2276	  The coherence hypothesis on \(\F\) is essential: a non-coherent \(\OO_X\)-module
2277	  need not become flat over any non-empty open of \(S\).
2278	\end{theorem}
2279	
2280	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "The above theorem has the following consequence, which
2281	% follows by restricting attention to a non-empty affine open subscheme of \(S\)."
2282	% (Source: nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1776-L1777. The
2283	% Nitsure proof is the single sentence quoted: deduce the geometric form from
2284	% the algebraic generic flatness statement (\cref{thm:generic_flatness_algebraic})
2285	% by restricting to a non-empty
2286	% affine open of \(S\), where the situation reduces to the algebraic statement.)
2287	\begin{proof}
2288	  \uses{thm:generic_flatness_algebraic, lem:gf_qcoh_fintype_finite_sections,
2289	        lem:gf_flat_locality_assembly}
2290	  Restrict to a non-empty affine open \(U_0 = \Spec A \subseteq S\), where \(A\) is a
2291	  noetherian domain (\(S\) integral and locally noetherian). We assemble the witness
2292	  \(V\) from the algebraic form on a finite affine cover.
2293	
2294	  \emph{Step 1 — finite affine cover above \(U_0\).} Since \(p\) is quasi-compact (by
2295	  the \([\mathrm{QuasiCompact}\,p]\) hypothesis) and \(U_0\) is affine hence
2296	  quasi-compact, the preimage \(X_{U_0} = p^{-1}(U_0)\) is quasi-compact; choose a
2297	  finite affine cover \(\{W_j = \Spec B_j\}_{j}\) of \(X_{U_0}\) by affine opens of
2298	  \(X\). Because \(p\) is locally of finite type, each ring \(B_j = \Gamma(X, W_j)\)
2299	  is a finite-type \(A\)-algebra.
2300	
2301	  \emph{Step 2 — read off a finite module on each patch.} On each \(W_j\), quasi-coherence
2302	  of \(\F\) identifies \(\F|_{W_j}\) with the associated sheaf \(\widetilde{M_j}\) of the
2303	  section module \(M_j = \Gamma(\F, W_j)\), and the finite-section bridge
2304	  \cref{lem:gf_qcoh_fintype_finite_sections} — applied to the affine open \(W_j\) — makes
2305	  \(M_j\) a finite \(B_j\)-module. Through the scalar tower \(A \to B_j \to M_j\),
2306	  \(M_j\) is the data fed to the algebraic form.
2307	
2308	  \emph{Step 3 — apply the algebraic form and take the common basic open.}
2309	  \cref{thm:generic_flatness_algebraic} applied to \((A, B_j, M_j)\) yields \(f_j \in A\),
2310	  \(f_j \ne 0\), with \((M_j)_{f_j}\) free over \(A_{f_j}\). As the cover is finite, set
2311	  \(f := \prod_j f_j \ne 0\) (a non-zero element of the domain \(A\)) and
2312	  \(V := D(f) \subseteq \Spec A \subseteq S\), a non-empty basic open; over \(V\) every
2313	  \((M_j)_f\) is free over \(A_f\).
2314	
2315	  \emph{Step 4 — freeness implies flatness.} On each patch the localized section module
2316	  is free, hence flat over \(A_f\); the flat-locality assembly
2317	  \cref{lem:gf_flat_locality_assembly} promotes these per-patch freeness statements to the
2318	  flatness conclusion by a \emph{source-span} descent: over any affine \(W \le p^{-1}(U)\)
2319	  one covers \(W\) by finitely many basic opens \(D(g) \subseteq W \cap W_j\) aligned to the
2320	  patches, propagates \(R\)-flatness from \((M_j)_f\) to each section module
2321	  \(\Gamma(\F, D(g))\) via the source-localization lemma, and descends along the spanning
2322	  family \(\{g\}\) of \(\Gamma(X, W)\) using \cref{lem:mathlib_flat_of_isLocalized_span}.
2323	  This produces the required \(V\) with \(\F|_{X_V}\) flat over \(\OO_V\).
2324	\end{proof}

2862	    {V U : S.Opens} (hV : IsAffineOpen V) (hU : IsAffineOpen U) (e : U ≤ V) :

`flat-iter054`: 2 must-fix-this-iter (sorry bodies) + 4 major findings (B2.3 type mismatch, 3 missing blueprinted declarations) + 3 minor invariant/private issues — 70 declarations checked, 5 red flags.

Report written to: `.archon/task_results/lean-vs-blueprint-checker-flat-iter054.md`

**Key finding on the directive's mismatch question**: Confirmed. The blueprint's `lem:gf_crossChart_spanning_cover` / `lem:gf_section_localization_twoleg` specify the stronger "restriction-matched pair `g|_O = ḡ|_O`" invariant. The Lean instead produces and uses only **basic-open equality `X.basicOpen g = X.basicOpen ḡ`** — documented intentionally in a NOTE inside `gf_common_basicOpen_basis` (the new iter-054 helper), because the restriction-matched invariant is not constructible via the cross-chart localization route. The weaker invariant is mathematically sufficient for all downstream uses (B2.2 only needs `D(g) = D(ḡ)` to identify the two section groups).
